---
title: "Leaflet_football_clubs"
author: "Simon Larsen and Jesper Jensen"
date: "2026-03-26"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


Introduction
In our project, we have chosen to create a map showing the locations of the 25 largest football clubs in Denmark. Our selection is based on which league the respective clubs will play in during the 2025/26 season. On this basis, we have included the 24 clubs competing in Denmark’s two top leagues—the Superliga and the 1st Division—plus Skive IK from the 2nd Division, Denmark’s third-highest tier. We included Skive IK because it is our childhood club, and adding it also brings the total to a round number.
In our spreadsheet, we have listed the following information about the 25 clubs: the club’s name, the name of its stadium, and the league in which the club competes. For the club’s specific location, we used the location of its stadium and recorded the corresponding longitude and latitude coordinates.



########################################  TASK NUMBER ONE


# Task 1: Create a Danish equivalent of AUSmap with Esri layers, 
# but call it DANmap. You will need it layer as a background for Danish data points.
```{r}

library(tidyverse)
library(googlesheets4)
library(leaflet)

esri <- grep("^Esri", providers, value = TRUE)


DANmap<- leaflet() %>%   # assign the base location to an object
  setView( 10.85089,55.2339084, zoom = 6.25) %>% 
  addTiles()



 DANmap1<- DANmap %>%
  addLayersControl(baseGroups = names(esri),
                   options = layersControlOptions(collapsed = FALSE)) %>%
  addMiniMap(tiles = esri[[1]], toggleDisplay = TRUE,
             position = "bottomright") %>%
  addMeasure(
    position = "bottomleft",
    primaryLengthUnit = "meters",
    primaryAreaUnit = "sqmeters",
    activeColor = "#3D535D",
    completedColor = "#7D4479") %>% 
  htmlwidgets::onRender("
                        function(el, x) {
                        var myMap = this;
                        myMap.on('baselayerchange',
                        function (e) {
                        myMap.minimap.changeLayer(L.tileLayer.provider(e.name));
                        })
                        }") %>% 
  addControl("", position = "topright")


 DANmap1
``` 
 



######################################## TASK TWO


# Task 2: Read in the googlesheet data you and your colleagues created
# into your DANmap object (with multiple background layers you created in Task 1).

# Solution
```{r}
# Libraries
install.packages("googlesheets4")
library(tidyverse)
library(googlesheets4)
library(leaflet)




football_stadiums <- read_sheet(
  "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1EcYYLqle10mE7E8Rbw1HfFMo0y7HX5HPJjb6hwNl0nw",
  sheet = "Sheet1",
  col_types = "cccnnc"
)



glimpse(football_stadiums)


football_stadiums %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Longitude)) %>% 
  filter(!is.na(Latitude))

football_stadiums <- football_stadiums |> 
  dplyr::rename(
    WrongLat = Latitude,
    WrongLon = Longitude
  ) |> 
  dplyr::mutate(
    Latitude = WrongLon,
    Longitude = WrongLat
  )



leaflet(football_stadiums) %>% 
  addTiles() %>% 
  addMarkers(
    lng = ~Longitude,
    lat = ~Latitude,
    popup = ~paste0(
      "<b>", Club, "</b><br>",
      "Stadium: ", Stadium, "<br>",
      "League: ", League
    )
  )
```

######################################## TASK THREE

# Task 3: Can you cluster the points in Leaflet?
# Hint: Google "clustering options in Leaflet in R"

# Solution
```{r}
leaflet(football_stadiums) %>% 
  addTiles() %>% 
  addMarkers(
    lng = ~Longitude,
    lat = ~Latitude,
    popup = ~paste0(
      "<b>", Club, "</b><br>",
      "Stadium: ", Stadium, "<br>",
      "League: ", League
    ),
    clusterOptions = markerClusterOptions()
  )
```
######################################## TASK FOUR

# Task 4: Look at the two maps (with and without clustering) and consider what
# each is good for and what not.
"The map with clustering is more pleasing to the eye,
because it reduces visual clutter and prevents markers from overlapping in areas
where many football clubs are located close together.
This creates a cleaner and more readable national overview,
especially in regions like Copenhagen or Aarhus where several clubs are concentrated.
However, clustering also requires the user to interact with the map
by zooming in to reveal the precise locations of individual stadiums.
In general, clustering is a strong choice when the goal is to present a large dataset
without overwhelming the viewer. It highlights regional patterns and makes the map
look more structured and professional. On the downside, clustering can hide
important details until the user zooms in, which means that exact stadium positions
are not immediately visible. For analytical tasks where precision matters,
a non‑clustered map may therefore be more appropriate, while clustering excels
when clarity and visual simplicity are the main priorities."



######################################## TASK FIVE

# Task 5: Find out how to display the notes and classifications column in the map. 
# Hint: Check online help in sites such as 
# https://r-charts.com/spatial/interactive-maps-leaflet/#popup

# Solution
```{r}
leaflet(football_stadiums) %>% 
  addTiles() %>% 
  addMarkers(
    lng = ~Longitude,
    lat = ~Latitude,
    popup = ~paste0(
      "<b>", Club, "</b><br>",
      "Stadium: ", Stadium, "<br>",
      "League: ", League, "<br>",
      "Notes: ", Notes, "<br>"
    ),
    clusterOptions = markerClusterOptions()
  )

```
######################################## CONGRATULATIONS - YOU ARE DONE :)
